q.en: Who goes on the fishing trip, and what is their relationship?
q.zh: 谁去钓鱼了？他们是什么关系？

options.en: Devon and his Grandpa; it is Devon's first time fishing with Grandpa, though his older brother Clay told him about it
options.zh: 德文(Devon)和他的爷爷；这是德文第一次跟爷爷钓鱼，不过哥哥克莱(Clay)早就跟他说过

options.en: Devon and his teacher on a school trip
options.zh: 德文和老师在学校的校外教学

options.en: Grandpa and Clay without Devon
options.zh: 爷爷和克莱，没有德文

options.en: Two strangers who just met at the river
options.zh: 两个在河边刚认识的陌生人

explain.en: Devon 'helped Grandpa maneuver the canoe,' and 'This was Devon's first time going fishing with Grandpa, but his older brother Clay had told him all about it.'
explain.zh: 德文「帮爷爷把独木舟划进河里」，而且「这是德文第一次跟爷爷钓鱼，不过哥哥克莱早就跟他说过这件事」。

q.en: What does Grandpa call 'the best catfish hole on the river,' and how do they reach it?
q.zh: 爷爷所说的「河上最好的鲶鱼点」是哪里？他们怎么到达那里的？

options.en: The spot near a big log where they pull the canoe onto the bank, after rounding a bend
options.zh: 绕过河弯后、靠近一根大木头、把独木舟拉上岸的那个点

options.en: The middle of a wide, fast waterfall
options.zh: 一道宽阔急瀑布的正中央

options.en: A spot right next to the country club
options.zh: 紧挨着乡村俱乐部的一处地方

options.en: The very place where the river begins
options.zh: 这条河的发源地

explain.en: As they rounded a bend, Grandpa said, 'This is the best catfish hole on the river,' and told Devon to jump out and pull the canoe up on land near the bank.
explain.zh: 绕过河弯时，爷爷说：「这是河上最好的鲶鱼点。」并让德文跳上岸，把独木舟拉到岸边。

q.en: An eagle appears several times and 'screeches' at the fishermen. What does the eagle add to the story?
q.zh: 一只鹰出现了好几次，对着钓鱼的人「尖叫」。这只鹰为故事增添了什么？

options.en: It follows them like a fellow fisherman, adding a lively wild companion and a sense of the river's setting
options.zh: 它像同伴一样跟着他们，为故事增添了一个活泼的野生伙伴，也烘托出河畔的氛围

options.en: It attacks Devon and tries to steal the canoe
options.zh: 它攻击德文，想抢走独木舟

options.en: It is a pet that belongs to Grandpa
options.zh: 它是爷爷养的宠物

options.en: It shows the river is dangerous and empty
options.zh: 它说明这条河危险又荒凉

explain.en: The eagle 'screeched' from a sycamore, then a willow, and Grandpa joked 'you're welcome to join us, but you have to catch your own fish' — it acts as a wild companion that mirrors their fishing trip.
explain.zh: 鹰先在一棵美国梧桐上「尖叫」，又在一棵柳树上叫，爷爷开玩笑说「欢迎你加入，但得自己抓鱼」——它像个野生伙伴，呼应着他们的钓鱼之行。

q.en: What warning did Devon's older brother Clay give him before the trip?
q.zh: 德文的哥哥克莱在出发前给了他什么提醒？

options.en: "Listen to what Grandpa says to do, and watch out for snakes!"
options.zh: 「听爷爷的话照做，还要当心蛇！」

options.en: "Never paddle on the right side of the canoe"
options.zh: 「永远别在独木舟右侧划桨」

options.en: "The eagle will carry you home"
options.zh: 「鹰会把你带回家」

options.en: "Bring lots of snacks for the fish"
options.zh: 「给鱼带很多零食」

explain.en: Clay had warned him, 'Listen to what Grandpa says to do,' and 'watch out for snakes!' — which is why Devon later worries about the log.
explain.zh: 克莱提醒他：「听爷爷的话照做」，还要「当心蛇！」——所以后来德文才会对着那根木头担心。

q.en: From what point of view is this story told?
q.zh: 这个故事是用什么视角（人称）叙述的？

options.en: Third-person — a narrator describes Devon's thoughts and feelings from outside
options.zh: 第三人称——叙述者从外部描写德文的想法和感受

options.en: First-person — Devon tells it himself as 'I'
options.zh: 第一人称——由德文以「我」的口吻讲述

options.en: Second-person — the reader is addressed as 'you'
options.zh: 第二人称——以「你」来称呼读者

options.en: It is written as a poem
options.zh: 它是一首诗的形式

explain.en: The narrator shows Devon's inner thoughts, e.g. 'Devon hoped Clay was teasing' and 'Devon swallowed hard' — describing him from outside, which is third-person.
explain.zh: 叙述者写出了德文的内心想法，例如「德文希望克莱是在逗他」、「德文使劲咽了口唾沫」——从外部描写他，是第三人称。

q.en: Devon is nervous about snakes because of Clay's story. What does the text show about whether the snake is really a danger?
q.zh: 因为克莱讲的故事，德文对蛇很紧张。文中说明了蛇到底是不是真的危险？

options.en: Grandpa says the old snake is 'long gone' (the 'gators got him), and Clay's tale may have been teasing — so the danger was mostly in Devon's imagination
options.zh: 爷爷说那条老蛇「早就不在了」（被短吻鳄吃了），而且克莱的故事可能只是逗他——所以危险多半是德文想象出来的

options.en: A snake really steals a catfish off Grandpa's hook every trip
options.zh: 每次出行都有蛇真的从爷爷钩上抢走鲶鱼

options.en: The snake lives in the canoe with them
options.zh: 蛇和他们一起住在独木舟里

options.en: Grandpa keeps the snake as a pet
options.zh: 爷爷把蛇当宠物养

explain.en: Combining clues: Clay 'told Devon' the snake story (Devon 'hoped Clay was teasing'), and Grandpa says the snake is 'long gone' — the threat was imagined, not real.
explain.zh: 把线索串起来：克莱「告诉德文」蛇的故事（德文「希望克莱是在逗他」），而爷爷说那条蛇「早就不在了」——威胁是想象出来的，并非真实。

q.en: Why do you think the author includes Clay's scary snake story?
q.zh: 你认为作者为什么要写克莱那个吓人的蛇故事？

options.en: To build suspense and show Devon's nervousness, making his relief when Grandpa reassures him more meaningful
options.zh: 为了制造悬念、表现德文的紧张，让他被爷爷安慰时的释然更有意味

options.en: To teach readers how to catch snakes
options.zh: 教读者怎样抓蛇

options.en: To prove the river has no wildlife
options.zh: 证明这条河里没有野生动物

options.en: To explain how catfish are born
options.zh: 讲解鲶鱼是怎么出生的

explain.en: The snake story makes Devon 'swallow hard' and worry about the log, building tension; Grandpa's calm 'you don't have to worry' then pays off that suspense.
explain.zh: 蛇的故事让德文「使劲咽唾沫」、对着木头担心，制造了紧张；爷爷一句安心的「你不必担心」，正好化解了这份悬念。

q.en: How do Grandpa and Devon get the canoe up on the riverbank at the fishing spot?
q.zh: 在钓鱼点，爷爷和德文是怎么把独木舟弄上岸的？

options.en: Grandpa steers to the bank, Devon jumps out, and Grandpa throws him a rope to pull the canoe onto land
options.zh: 爷爷把船划向岸边，德文跳上岸，爷爷把绳子扔给他，他把船拉到陆地上

options.en: They lift the whole canoe overhead and carry it
options.zh: 他们把整条独木舟举过头顶扛着走

options.en: A passing motorboat tows them in
options.zh: 路过的摩托艇把他们拖上岸

options.en: The eagle flies it to shore
options.zh: 鹰把船飞到了岸上

explain.en: Grandpa said, 'I want you to jump out, and I'll throw you the rope to pull the canoe up on land.' Devon 'jumped out... Grandpa threw him the rope, and Devon tugged the boat onto the riverbank.'
explain.zh: 爷爷说：「我想让你跳上岸，我把绳子扔给你，你把独木舟拉到陆地上。」德文「跳下船……爷爷把绳子扔给他，德文把船拖上了河岸。」

q.en: Grandpa says 'The 'gators got him' about the snake, and then winks. What does the wink tell the reader?
q.zh: 爷爷说蛇「被短吻鳄吃了」，然后眨了眨眼。这个眨眼告诉读者什么？

options.en: Grandpa is joking and teasing — the snake tale is playful, meant to calm Devon with humor, not to be taken literally
options.zh: 爷爷是在开玩笑、逗他——蛇的故事是闹着玩的，用幽默让德文安心，不必当真

options.en: Grandpa is frightened of alligators for real
options.zh: 爷爷真的害怕短吻鳄

options.en: The snake will return tomorrow
options.zh: 那条蛇明天会回来

options.en: Devon should jump back into the water
options.zh: 德文应该跳回水里

explain.en: Grandpa first says the snake is 'long gone,' then adds 'The 'gators got him,' and 'Grandpa winked' — the wink shows he is teasing, using humor to reassure Devon.
explain.zh: 爷爷先说蛇「早就不在了」，又补一句「被短吻鳄吃了」，并「眨了眨眼」——眨眼说明他在逗德文，用幽默来安抚他。

q.en: What feeling does the story mainly leave the reader with about the fishing trip?
q.zh: 这个钓鱼的故事主要给读者留下怎样的感受？

options.en: Warmth and closeness between Devon and Grandpa, and the quiet joy of a first adventure in nature
options.zh: 德文和爷爷之间的温暖亲近，以及第一次自然冒险的宁静喜悦

options.en: Fear that the river is a dangerous, scary place
options.zh: 害怕这条河是个危险可怕的地方

options.en: Boredom because nothing happens
options.zh: 因为什么都没发生而感到无聊

options.en: Anger that Devon was forced to go
options.zh: 对德文被迫前往的愤怒

explain.en: The calm canoe glide 'as silently as a leaf,' Grandpa's gentle teasing, and the final 'Let's catch ourselves a big catfish' leave a warm, fond tone about a special grandfather-grandson outing.
explain.zh: 独木舟「像叶子飘落一样无声」地滑过、爷爷温柔的逗趣，以及结尾「我们来钓条大鲶鱼吧」，都留下祖孙二人温馨出行的基调。

prompt.en: Devon was nervous about snakes because of his brother's story, but Grandpa reassured him with calm words and a joke. Think of a time an older family member or friend helped you face a fear or try something new. What happened, and what did you learn about asking for help or using humor?
prompt.zh: 德文因为哥哥讲的故事而对蛇很紧张，但爷爷用平静的话和一个玩笑安抚了他。回想一次有年长的家人或朋友帮你面对恐惧、或尝试新事物的经历。发生了什么？你从「求助」或「用幽默化解」中学到了什么？

en: Clay warned Devon to 'watch out for snakes!' and told of a snake stealing a catfish off the hook (p. 7).
Grandpa reassured him: 'you don't have to worry about that old snake... that old snake is long gone,' then winked (pp. 10-11).
A good answer tells a real personal story and reflects on how kindness or humor can ease a fear.
zh: 克莱提醒德文「当心蛇！」，还讲了一条蛇从钩上抢走鲶鱼的事（第7页）。
爷爷安慰他：「你不必担心那条老蛇……它早就不在了」，然后眨了眨眼（第10-11页）。
好的回答要讲一个真实的亲身故事，并反思善意或幽默如何能化解恐惧。
